Day 11 — Oil crashes 11% on Trump 'war ending soon'
Day 12 — IEA 400M barrel release; oil rebounds 3%
Day 13 — AAA avg $3.58, CA above $5.00. Oil at $100+
$3.63 — up 35¢ in one week. California above $5/gal. Analysts predict $4/gal nationally next week.
Day 15 — IEA 400M barrel release fails to stem prices
Day 16 PM — AAA average $3.70, up 23% since Feb 28
Day 17 — Dubai airport struck, Fujairah oil hub hit again
Day 17 — Hormuz zero-transit milestone
Day 18 — Shah gas field ablaze; oil surges; gas hits $3.79
Day 19 — Gas spikes to $3.84; CA $5.53; Fed holds rates; highest since Oct 2023
Day 20 - Gulf energy attacks
Day 20 PM — AAA avg $3.88; CA $5.62; up 28¢ in one week
Day 21 (Nowruz) — AAA national average $3.88; Bessent plans 140M barrel Iranian crude release
Day 21 Evening — AAA: $3.912/gal (+28¢ week-over-week); CA $5.61; US Treasury formally lifts sanctions on 140M barrels Iranian crude
Day 22 (Nowruz) — AAA: $3.925/gal (+23.4%, +$0.745 from pre-war); Brent at $110.02; Natanz struck again. CA $5.53; KS $3.15.
Day 23 (Trump ultimatum)
Day 23 (Trump ultimatum)
Day 24 — Trump 5-day pause on energy strikes; Iran denies talks; WTI slides to $91.40
Day 25 — AAA avg $3.977, up $1.02/gal from one month ago; approaching $4/gal milestone. CA $5.79. Diesel $5.29. Market watching Trump 5-day energy-strike pause.

Day 26 — AAA avg holds $3.977; Brent dropped to $99.75 on Trump Iran-talks signals; Iran rejected US 15-point plan; Saudi/UAE moving toward war entry; Philippines declared energy emergency; pump prices lag oil by 2-3 days
Day 27 — Brent surges to $108; Trump extends energy pause to April 6; AAA $3.981/gal
Day 28 PM — Brent $112.57 (war high, +41%); WTI crosses $100; Hormuz blockade Day 25; Wave 84; cluster bomb kills 1 in Israel; $4.00/gal milestone approaching
Day 29 — Houthis enter war; Kuwait Airport radar damaged; Islamabad peace summit; Trump calls NATO 'paper tiger'; Brent $112.57 (war high +41%); WTI $99.64 (+32%); $4.00 milestone imminent

Day 30 PM — IDF evening Tehran wave + power blackouts; IAEA confirms Khondab destroyed; UNIFIL peacekeeper killed; Trump Kharg Island threat; Islamabad summit concludes; Iran NPT withdrawal bill; $3.981/gal (+25.2%)

Day 31 AM — IDF 120+ bombs Tehran; Tabriz Petrochemical hit; Haifa Bazan refinery fire; Trump 'regime change achieved' + Kharg Island seizure threat; Spain closes airspace; Brent $114.90/bbl; $4.00/gal milestone imminent

Day 31 PM — AAA avg hits $3.99/gal (+$0.87 from pre-war); WTI closes above $100 for first time since July 2022; Brent settles $112.78 (intraday $116 on Trump Kharg Island threat); USS Tripoli arrives; 3 UNIFIL peacekeepers killed

Day 32 AM — AAA national avg crosses $4.00 at $4.02/gal (+$1.04 from Feb 26 $2.98); first time above $4 since Aug 2022; up $0.10 in one week; California $5.87/gal; Diesel $5.45/gal; Brent $112.90/bbl; Iran Parliament Hormuz toll plan; Al-Salmi tanker attack
Day 32 PM — AAA $4.018/gal — crosses $4 milestone. First since Aug 2022. Up 26.4% from pre-war $3.18.
Day 33 AM — AAA avg holding at $4.018/gal. Brent seesawing $101-$108 on Trump exit talk. CA $5.89/gal. Diesel $5.45/gal. EIA weekly: $4.126.

Day 33 PM — AAA avg holds $4.018/gal. Oil crashing (Brent ~$102, WTI <$100) on Trump exit signals — pump prices may lag oil decline 2-3 days. IEA: 'April worse.' Ceasefire by April 6: $3.50-$3.75; escalation: $5-$7 (Time).
Day 33 night — $4.06/gal (AAA). First above $4 since Aug 2022. Diesel $5.49. California $5.89. Oklahoma $3.25. Brent spiked to $106 on Trump address. April 6 Hormuz deadline may push higher.
Day 34 PM — $4.08/gal (AAA). Up 28.3% from pre-war $3.18. Brent surging $109.35 (+37%). 40-nation Hormuz summit. Iran-Oman navigation regime draft. OPEC+ Apr 5. DOE SPR bids due Apr 6.

Day 35 — $4.09/gal (AAA). Up 28.6% from pre-war $3.18. Brent $109.03. WTI $111.54 (WTI-Brent inversion). Kuwait refinery+desal plant hit. Iran claims F-15E shootdown. 130 Hezbollah rockets. April 6 deadline 3 days.
Day 35 afternoon — AAA $4.09/gal (first above $4 since Aug 2022). Diesel $5.43. CA $5.89.

Day 36 AM — $4.091 (AAA Apr 3 latest reading). Iran 15 missile impacts in Israel. 19 Iraqi militia attacks on US bases. Ghalibaf Bab el-Mandeb threat. Brent $109.24 (-2.8% from $112.42). UNSC vote Apr 5. April 6 deadline 2 DAYS.
Day 37 — $4.104 (+1.3¢ on day). AAA: first above $4/gal in 4 years. Bushehr 4th strike. Mahshahr petrochem hit. Kuwait oil complex droned. Iran rejects ultimatum. April 6 deadline TOMORROW.

Day 38 — $4.104 (flat on day). Sunday. Haifa 2 dead/2 missing. Cluster bombs Petah Tikva+Haifa. Lebanon 11 killed. IRGC 'new Gulf order.' OPEC+ +206k bpd May. Iran rejects deadline. April 7 deadline TOMORROW.
Day 38 EVE — $4.10/gal; Trump threatens total destruction; deadline TOMORROW
Day 38 Night — $4.119/gal; Russia-Iran intel sharing; Velayati threatens Bab al-Mandeb; Vance ceasefire talks; Brent $111.25; deadline TOMORROW 8PM ET
Day 39 AM — DEADLINE DAY. ~$4.12/gal. Hegseth: most intense strikes today. Trump 8pm ET deadline TONIGHT. Iran rejects ceasefire. Brent $110.05. WTI $113.42.

Day 39 PM — DEADLINE DAY. Gas $4.14 (+30.2%). Brent $111.20/WTI $115.21. US 50+ strikes Kharg Island. IDF bridges/railways. Trump: 'civilization will die tonight.' CA $5.89. CA diesel $7.52. SF first city >$7 diesel. OPEC+ +206K bpd May.

Day 39 EVE — DEADLINE HOUR. Gas $4.14 (+30.2%). Brent $113.40 (+42.0%). WTI $115.17 (+52.5%). Russia/China veto Hormuz. Iran cuts US diplomacy. IRGC hits Saudi Jubail. Khamenei incapacitated. 8PM ET deadline NOW.
Day 40 AM — CEASEFIRE. Gas $4.119 (+29.5%). Brent rebounded from $93 crash to $109.27. WTI $112.75. Iran SNSC accepts ceasefire. Hormuz conditional reopening.

Day 40 PM — Gas $4.14 (+30.2%). Up 2¢ in 24h. Brent stabilized $95.80 (+20.0%). WTI $97.28. Iran violated ceasefire with missiles then complied. Netanyahu: Lebanon excluded. Analysts: gas could drop below $4 in 1-2 weeks if ceasefire holds.

Day 40 — CEASEFIRE. AAA average $4.14/gal (+30.2%). Pump prices lag crude 1-2 weeks. Oil crashed 14-16% on ceasefire but gas unchanged yet. Analysts: could fall below $4 within 1-2 weeks if ceasefire holds and Hormuz reopens.

formula: Pump Price = (Crude Oil ÷ 42 gallons) + Refining + Distribution + Federal Tax + State Tax + Retail Margin

preWar.label: Before War (Feb 27)
preWar.note: Actual retail average was $3.18 (includes regional variation)

current.label: Current (Apr 8)
current.note: National average $4.14/gal (AAA April 8 PM). Up 2¢ in 24h. Brent $95.80 (+20.0%). WTI $97.28 (+28.8%). Oil crashed 16% on ceasefire, stabilizing. Analysts: could fall below $4 in 1-2 weeks if ceasefire holds. Expert cautions 'do little or nothing' short-term.

context: Day 40 CEASEFIRE: AAA national average $4.14/gal — up 30.2% from pre-war $3.18. Up 2¢ in 24h. Two-week ceasefire in effect. Brent crashed to ~$93, stabilized at $95.80. WTI $97.28. Lavan refinery struck post-ceasefire; Iran retaliates vs Gulf states. First Hormuz transits. Analysts: gas could fall below $4 in 1-2 weeks if ceasefire holds.
